data.body: Reopened to certify its closure (it was previously closed WITHOUT a completion signature, blocking the epic's child-closure check). The work is delivered: (1) DISABLE-CONFIG / kill-switch = the verify.require_plan_review_for_claim config preference (default OFF; turning it off IS the rollback — an ordinary preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch, per the epic's own clarification), implemented in config.py + the claim precheck (092b) and documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md (Configuration / 'turning it off is the rollback'). (2) ROLLOUT-ORDERING for the signature scheme = the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar follows rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout (NOT in KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES; in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES so fsck recognizes it; older clones preserve-and-ignore until upgrade — upgrade reconcile hosts first), and the plan-review attestation REUSES the existing SIGNATURE event (no new signing event type to roll out). db7b + reducer/_version + event_append cover this.
